title: Cisco Nexus Dashboard Insights Advisory, Field Notice, PSIRT, and Bug-Match Tracking with Compliance Window Reporting
exclusions: Cisco Catalyst 9000 / 8000 / 8500 / Catalyst Center compliance — use the dedicated Cisco Catalyst Center use cases under cat-15 (Cisco Catalyst Center has its own PSIRT/Field-Notice integration); Cisco UCS hardware advisory tracking (use cat-19 — UCS Manager has its own EOL/Field-Notice telemetry); ad-hoc PSIRT advisory ingestion without correlation to deployed inventory (no NDI / CMDB inventory baseline = false-positive advisory storm); Cisco Secure-Workload / Tetration policy compliance (different domain — cat-04); third-party SAST/SCA bug-tracking (Snyk, Black Duck, Sonatype) for application code (different domain — cat-08).
dataSources: `index=cisco_dc` (or `cisco_network`, `network_operations`, `cisco_operations`) combining **`sourcetype=cisco:ndi:psirt`** / **`sourcetype=cisco:ndi:bug`** / **`sourcetype=cisco:ndi:advisory`** JSON from **NDI** External Streaming → Splunk **HTTP Event Collector**, plus **`sourcetype=cisco:psirt:advisory`** JSON from Cisco **PSIRT openVuln API** (`https://api.cisco.com/security/advisories/v2/all`) via **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, Splunkbase **1546**) using **OAuth 2.0** **client_credentials** tokens from **`https://id.cisco.com/oauth2/default/v1/token`**, **`sourcetype=cisco:fieldnotice:advisory`** from **`https://api.cisco.com/field-notices/v1/all`**, **`sourcetype=cisco:eol:bulletin`** from **`https://api.cisco.com/software/eol/v2/lifecycle/products`**, optional ServiceNow CMDB joins (`cmdb_ci`, software asset tables), and **`sourcetype=cisco:dc_networking_app:dashboard`** extracts surfaced inside **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** (**Splunkbase 7777**) compliance dashboards.
app: **`cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk`** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) ships **PSIRT Match Trend**, **Field Notice Trend**, **Compliance Rule Violation Trend** dashboards atop **`cisco:ndi:*`** advisory sourcetypes—pair with **`Splunk REST API Modular Input`** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for **OAuth**-authenticated pulls against Cisco **PSIRT**, Field Notice, and **EoX** endpoints plus Splunk Help **HTTP Event Collector** guidance for **NDI** webhook exports.
spl:
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=cisco_operations OR index=network_operations)
(sourcetype="cisco:ndi:psirt" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:bug" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:advisory"
 OR sourcetype="cisco:psirt:advisory" OR sourcetype="cisco:fieldnotice:advisory" OR sourcetype="cisco:eol:bulletin")
earliest=-30d@d
| eval evt_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(eventType, event_type, advisory_kind, "")))
| eval adv_key=trim(coalesce(advisoryId, advisory_id, fieldNoticeNumber, fn_id, bulletin_key, ""))
| eval cve_tag=upper(trim(coalesce(cveId, cve_id, "")))
| eval cvss_val=tonumber(coalesce(cvssBaseScore, cvss_score, cvssScore, ""))
| eval sev_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(severity, Severity, riskTier, "")))
| eval first_seen=strptime(trim(coalesce(firstPublished, first_published, firstPublishedDate, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval last_touch=strptime(trim(coalesce(lastUpdated, last_updated, lastModified, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval bug_token=trim(coalesce(bugId, bug_id, softwareBugId, ""))
| eval prod_line=mvjoin(coalesce(productNames, product_names, iosNxAffected, affectedReleases), " | ")
| eval site_scope=mvjoin(coalesce(affectedSites, ndi_site_list, fabricSite, siteName), ",")
| eval fix_train=mvjoin(coalesce(firstFixedReleases, first_fixed, remediationRelease, ""), ",")
| rex field=bug_token "(?i)(?<csc>CSC[a-z]{2,5}[0-9]{5,})"
| eval fn_marker=if(match(adv_key,"(?i)^FN-[0-9]+"),1,0)
| eval psirt_marker=if(match(lower(adv_key),"cisco-sa-"),1,0)
| eval eox_marker=if(evt_lc IN ("eox","eos","lifecycle","ldos") OR sourcetype="cisco:eol:bulletin",1,0)
| where evt_lc IN ("psirt","bug","advisory","field_notice","lifecycle","") OR psirt_marker=1 OR fn_marker=1 OR isnotnull(cve_tag)
| where sev_lc IN ("critical","high","medium","low","informational") OR (isnotnull(cvss_val) AND cvss_val>=3.1)
| stats latest(_time) as last_evidence_time,
        earliest(first_seen) as earliest_publish,
        latest(last_touch) as newest_revision,
        values(cve_tag) as cve_bundle,
        max(cvss_val) as cvss_high_water,
        values(bug_token) as csc_candidates,
        dc(site_scope) as footprint_sites,
        values(prod_line) as nx_os_product_strings,
        latest(fix_train) as remediation_hint,
        latest(publicationUrl) as vendor_hyperlink
    by adv_key, sev_lc
| eval urgency_lane=case(
    cvss_high_water>=9.0 OR sev_lc="critical", "cab_review_same_week",
    cvss_high_water>=7.0 OR sev_lc="high", "vendor_mgmt_escalation",
    fn_marker=1 AND match(lower(nx_os_product_strings), "(?i)nexus.?9"), "field_notice_hardware_track",
    eox_marker=1, "eos_ldos_calendar_watch",
    1=1, "weekly_digest_row")
| sort -cvss_high_water, -last_evidence_time
| head 400
description: Tracks Cisco **PSIRT** vulnerability disclosures, Field Notice bulletins (**FN-#####** hardware/software/documentation cadences), **CSC** software-fault identifiers, **EoX/EOS** milestones (**Last Date of Support**, **End of Sale**), and **NDI** advisory-window evaluations (`eventType=psirt`, `eventType=bug`) alongside **`cvssBaseScore`**, **`cveId`**, **`advisoryId`**, **`affectedReleases[]`**, **`firstFixedReleases[]`**, **`productNames[]`**, **`iosNxAffected[]`**, **`cwes[]`**, **`publicationUrl`**, **`firstSeenInSite`**, **`affectedSites[]`**, and compliance scorecard deltas—correlating Cisco **openVuln API**, **Field Notice API**, **EoX API**, and **NDI** webhook feeds so vendor management, CAB, and compliance teams manage risk on **daily/weekly** cadences rather than live anomaly telemetry streams.
value: Consolidating **PSIRT**, Field Notice, bug-database, lifecycle, and advisory-window evidence in Splunk collapses duplicate **advisoryId** counts, exposes **CVE/** **CVSS** exposure against real NX-OS builds, ties **FN-72366**/**FN-70816**/**FN-72088** style notices to racks on the floor, and pairs **Splunkbase 7777** **PSIRT Match Trend** visuals with immutable **OAuth**-backed pulls from Cisco developer APIs so **mean-time-to-patch**, advisory-to-resolution, and **Last Date of Support** milestones stay inside governance SLAs without waiting for quarterly manual spreadsheet reconciliations.
implementation: Enable **NDI** advisory **HEC** streams for **`eventType=psirt`**/**`eventType=bug`**, schedule **rest_ta** collectors for Cisco **PSIRT**/**Field Notice**/**EoX** endpoints with DevNet credentials, enrich with CMDB normalized release strings, macro-map **severity**/**`cvssBaseScore`** thresholds to **weekly** CAB reports and optional **daily** critical slices, and pin **Splunkbase 7777** dashboard evidence for governance stakeholders.
visualization: (1) **Splunkbase 7777** — **PSIRT Match Trend** with **`cvss_high_water`** overlays; (2) **7777** — **Field Notice Trend** highlighting **FN-#####** families; (3) **7777** — **Compliance Rule Violation Trend** filtered to advisory-window evaluation spikes; (4) Timechart — count of distinct **`adv_key`** by **`urgency_lane`** across **weekly** buckets; (5) Table — **`adv_key`**, **`cve_tag`**, **`cvss_high_water`**, **`footprint_sites`**, **`csc_candidates`**, **`vendor_hyperlink`** for remediation owners.

references.title: Cisco Nexus Dashboard Insights User Guide, Release 6.4
Cisco DC Networking Application for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)

knownFalsePositives: **PSIRT republication** or amended disclosures that bump **`lastUpdated`** while keeping the same **`advisoryId`** can double-count backlog metrics if Splunk rolls up only on **`_time`** instead of grouping on **`adv_key` + `newest_revision`**. **NDI** bug-database **weekly** synchronization (commonly clustered on a recurring weekday per operations lore) projects a burst of “new” **`CSC`** rows that merely reflect refreshed metadata—not fresh exposure—until week-over-week deltas settle. **NX-OS** train strings rendered as **`9.3(13)`** vs **`9.3.13`** vs **`9.3.13.G(0.1)`** fool naive string equality when correlating **`affectedReleases[]`** membership against CMDB normalization rules, yielding false negatives even while **NDI** already flagged a match internally. **Field Notice** **EOL** banners that align to **calendar-quarter** reporting windows can spike trend charts unrelated to urgent optics swaps—tie **`FN-#####`** escalations to rack elevation proof rather than arbitrary midnight thresholds alone. **CMDB** software-asset inventories lagging physical decommission steps produce **PSIRT** hits referencing chassis already retired—suppress pages until nightly reconciliation clears stale serial-to-release tuples. **NDI** quarterly advisory-window bundle imports may temporarily flood **Compliance Rule Violation Trend** charts until evaluator cycles finish—delay ticketing until **`newest_revision`** timestamps plateau instead of reacting during the ingest spike alone.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Stand up Nexus Dashboard Insights builds documented for **Release 6.4** onward—size **NDI** Sites against **APIC**, **NDFC**, or standalone **NX-OS** inventories before expecting advisory matches sourced from proactive Cisco disclosure feeds rather than instantaneous fault consoles.
• Register a Cisco DevNet application so **OAuth 2.0** **client_credentials** flows against `https://id.cisco.com/oauth2/default/v1/token` mint bearer tokens for `https://api.cisco.com/security/advisories/v2/all`, `https://api.cisco.com/field-notices/v1/all`, and `https://api.cisco.com/software/eol/v2/lifecycle/products` without scattering secrets outside Splunk **credential store** references handled through **deployment server** bundles.
• Install Splunk REST API Modular Input (**rest_ta**, **Splunkbase 1546**) on a **Heavy Forwarder** authorized for outbound HTTPS; complement with HTTP Event Collector endpoints documented in **Splunk Help** so **NDI** **External Streaming** can emit **`eventType=psirt`** and **`eventType=bug`** payloads tagged **`cisco:ndi:psirt`** and **`cisco:ndi:bug`** separately from anomaly telemetry streams emphasized elsewhere.
• Deploy `cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk` (**Splunkbase 7777**) to unlock **PSIRT Match Trend**, **Field Notice Trend**, and Compliance Rule Violation Trend workspaces that anchor **CAB** slide decks beside raw indexes carrying **`advisoryId`** and advisory-window narratives.
• Align **LDAP**/**SAML** **Site Manager** versus **Approver** boundaries with Splunk roles so webhook-only Sites never leak cross-tenant advisory rows into shared **`index=cisco_dc`** shards monitored by vendor-risk reviewers.

Step 1 — Wire collection paths for governance telemetry
(1) NDI webhook exports: paste Splunk **HEC** URIs (`https://<splunk>/services/collector/event`) into **NDI** streaming panes—stamp sourcetypes **`cisco:ndi:psirt`**, **`cisco:ndi:bug`**, `cisco:ndi:advisory` and verify JSON carries **`advisoryId`**, **`cveId`**, **`cvssBaseScore`**, **`severity`**, **`affectedSites[]`**, **`firstSeenInSite`**, **`publicationUrl`**, and **`lastModified`** fields cited in Cisco documentation for proactive disclosure workflows tied to OAuth-backed collectors.
(2) PSIRT openVuln pulls: configure **rest_ta** GET requests with query parameters such as `severity=critical` plus `product=Cisco Nexus 9000 Series Switches`—normalize arrays **`productNames[]`**, **`iosNxAffected[]`**, `affectedReleases[]`, `firstFixedReleases[]`, **`cwes[]`** into `cisco:psirt:advisory` events for correlation with **CMDB** trains expressed as **NX-OS** strings including dotted and parenthetical releases.
(3) Field Notice pulls: route **Field Notice API** JSON into `cisco:fieldnotice:advisory`—track identifiers like **`FN-72366`**, **`FN-70816`**, **`FN-72088`**, categories (**Hardware**, **Software**, **Performance**, **Documentation**, **End-of-Life**), and optics narratives separate from instantaneous fault telemetry emphasized under sibling anomaly monitoring use cases.
(4) EoX lifecycle pulls: mirror phases (**End of Sale**, Last Date of Support, End of Service Renewal) into `cisco:eol:bulletin` so governance cadences capture **NX-OS**, **ACI**, **NDI**, and **Nexus Dashboard** milestones referenced in quarterly vendor reviews alongside **CVE** scoring overlays.
(5) ServiceNow enrichment: optionally stream summarized hits through **HEC** into **CMDB** staging tables—map **`affectedReleases`** tokens against normalized image strings (`9.3(13)` versus **`9.3.13.G(0.1)`**) via lookup tables maintained by infrastructure owners responsible for advisory-to-resolution SLAs.

Step 2 — Promote saved searches from the SPL statement
Keep the authoritative SPL inside the **`spl`** JSON field for saved searches—paste that block into Search, validate field coverage, then schedule **weekly** executions with optional **daily** tier-one slices filtered on `severity=critical` or `cvss_high_water>=9`.

```spl
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=cisco_operations OR index=network_operations)
(sourcetype="cisco:ndi:psirt" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:bug" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:advisory"
 OR sourcetype="cisco:psirt:advisory" OR sourcetype="cisco:fieldnotice:advisory" OR sourcetype="cisco:eol:bulletin")
earliest=-30d@d
| eval evt_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(eventType, event_type, advisory_kind, "")))
| eval adv_key=trim(coalesce(advisoryId, advisory_id, fieldNoticeNumber, fn_id, bulletin_key, ""))
| eval cve_tag=upper(trim(coalesce(cveId, cve_id, "")))
| eval cvss_val=tonumber(coalesce(cvssBaseScore, cvss_score, cvssScore, ""))
| eval sev_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(severity, Severity, riskTier, "")))
| eval first_seen=strptime(trim(coalesce(firstPublished, first_published, firstPublishedDate, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval last_touch=strptime(trim(coalesce(lastUpdated, last_updated, lastModified, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval bug_token=trim(coalesce(bugId, bug_id, softwareBugId, ""))
| eval prod_line=mvjoin(coalesce(productNames, product_names, iosNxAffected, affectedReleases), " | ")
| eval site_scope=mvjoin(coalesce(affectedSites, ndi_site_list, fabricSite, siteName), ",")
| eval fix_train=mvjoin(coalesce(firstFixedReleases, first_fixed, remediationRelease, ""), ",")
| rex field=bug_token "(?i)(?<csc>CSC[a-z]{2,5}[0-9]{5,})"
| eval fn_marker=if(match(adv_key,"(?i)^FN-[0-9]+"),1,0)
| eval psirt_marker=if(match(lower(adv_key),"cisco-sa-"),1,0)
| eval eox_marker=if(evt_lc IN ("eox","eos","lifecycle","ldos") OR sourcetype="cisco:eol:bulletin",1,0)
| where evt_lc IN ("psirt","bug","advisory","field_notice","lifecycle","") OR psirt_marker=1 OR fn_marker=1 OR isnotnull(cve_tag)
| where sev_lc IN ("critical","high","medium","low","informational") OR (isnotnull(cvss_val) AND cvss_val>=3.1)
| stats latest(_time) as last_evidence_time,
        earliest(first_seen) as earliest_publish,
        latest(last_touch) as newest_revision,
        values(cve_tag) as cve_bundle,
        max(cvss_val) as cvss_high_water,
        values(bug_token) as csc_candidates,
        dc(site_scope) as footprint_sites,
        values(prod_line) as nx_os_product_strings,
        latest(fix_train) as remediation_hint,
        latest(publicationUrl) as vendor_hyperlink
    by adv_key, sev_lc
| eval urgency_lane=case(
    cvss_high_water>=9.0 OR sev_lc="critical", "cab_review_same_week",
    cvss_high_water>=7.0 OR sev_lc="high", "vendor_mgmt_escalation",
    fn_marker=1 AND match(lower(nx_os_product_strings), "(?i)nexus.?9"), "field_notice_hardware_track",
    eox_marker=1, "eos_ldos_calendar_watch",
    1=1, "weekly_digest_row")
| sort -cvss_high_water, -last_evidence_time
| head 400
```

Stage rationale:
• `adv_key`** merges **`advisoryId`** values with **`FN-#####` tokens—stable across amended disclosures where `lastUpdated` moves faster than `cve_tag` text mirrored from Cisco PSIRT openVuln API** payloads.
• **`cvss_high_water`** prioritizes **CAB escalation whenever `cvssBaseScore` crests—even if textual `severity` labels lag portal typography referenced during CVSS** comparisons.
• **`csc_candidates`** surfaces **`CSCwd123456` identifiers echoed from NDI bug-database synchronization jobs executed on weekly cadences described for NDI 6.4+ per-site customization guidance.
• `footprint_sites` counts impacted Sites for vendor-management burn-down charts tied to mean-time-to-patch KPIs—not streaming dataplane alarms tracked under separate anomaly workflows.

Step 3 — Evidence checks beside Cisco consoles
(1) Spot-check `adv_key`** rows inside **NDI advisory panes—confirm `bugId`** parity with **`CSCwd123456` strings Splunk extracted via `rex` helpers tuned for CSC identifiers.
(2) Compare `cve_tag` entries against Cisco PSIRT portal URLs referenced through `publicationUrl` fields—truncated proxy payloads usually omit trailing `cwes[]` arrays first even though amended disclosures refreshed `lastUpdated` timestamps.
(3) Validate `FN-72366`**/**`FN-72088` optics narratives against Field Notice PDFs—Splunk should mirror severity intent even when optics wording evolves across republications counted once per `advisoryId` baseline.
(4) Compare EoX** **`Last Date of Support` counters with finance-led spreadsheets—Splunk trending proves lifecycle drift detection independent from spreadsheet edits tied to EOS milestones.
(5) Capture Splunkbase 7777** **PSIRT Match Trend screenshots quarterly for auditors reviewing advisory backlog posture versus dashboards centered on instantaneous fabric events.

Step 4 — Operational rhythm for risk stakeholders
(1) Publish weekly** **CAB-ready CSV extracts—reserve daily notifications for `urgency_lane=cab_review_same_week`** lanes carrying **`cvss_high_water>=9` only so alerting cadence stays slow-moving versus telemetry-heavy anomaly paging paths.
(2) Route `vendor_mgmt_escalation` lanes to procurement forums tracking `FN-#####` commitments separately from CVE remediation SLAs referenced inside PSIRT disclosures.
(3) Pair Splunk Enterprise Security adaptive responses with ServiceNow** **`CHG` templates referencing `advisoryId` strings verbatim—avoid paraphrasing Cisco disclosure titles during forensic replay exercises mandated by governance teams.
(4) Subscribe analysts to `https://sec.cloudapps.cisco.com/security/center/psirtrss20/CiscoPSIRT.xml` for headline alerts while Splunk retains immutable metrics tied to `firstPublished`** and **`lastUpdated` columns exported through rest_ta jobs.
(5) Document advisory-window evaluations whenever Cisco ships curated governance bundles inside NDI quarterly drops—annotate dashboards explaining drift windows versus instantaneous snapshots tied to baseline posture statements.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting modes that masquerade as fresh exposure
• OAuth `401`** responses from **`api.cisco.com`** —rotate DevNet **`client_id`**/**`client_secret` pairs and confirm Splunk collectors honor TLS 1.2 cipher suites mandated upstream for Cisco PSIRT** integrations.
• **`Duplicate advisoryId` storms after republication —collapse `stats`** using **`adv_key`** plus **`newest_revision`**, **not** **`_time` alone, so amended disclosures tied to Cisco PSIRT republication cycles do not multiply backlog KPIs unintentionally.
• Weekly Tuesday bug-database refreshes** inside **NDI —temporary spikes resemble incidents; compare `last_evidence_time` week-over-week before paging executives expecting instantaneous incident semantics from governance telemetry.
• Version-token mismatches (`9.3(13)` versus `9.3.13.G(0.1)`**) —extend **`EVAL`**/**`FIELDALIAS`** chains until **`affectedReleases[]` comparisons behave identically to NDI internal parsers cited inside Cisco advisory tooling guidance.
• Calendar-quarter Field Notice bursts —hardware lifecycle banners align with fiscal reporting—not necessarily urgent optic swaps—tie `FN-#####` alerts to rack elevation proof before downtime approvals referencing optics-field narratives such as `FN-72088` micro-movement themes.
• CMDB lag versus live switches** —**PSIRT hits referencing retired serials inflate exposure until nightly reconciliation completes—suppress using `inventory_age` lookups sourced from CMDB exports refreshed faster than quarterly spreadsheets alone.
• Advisory-window bundle ingestion** —fresh **Compliance Rule Violation Trend** spikes after **NDI upgrades usually settle once evaluator cycles finish—pause ticketing until `newest_revision` timestamps plateau following quarterly Cisco rule refreshes documented under Nexus Dashboard Insights** cadences.
• **`rest_ta` HTTP `429` saturation —shard `openVuln` filters per product family per Cisco developer throttling guidance instead of single mega-polls requesting entire advisory catalogs hourly without backoff macros.
• HEC misroutes —tokens scoped to `index=cisco_lab` silently drop production `eventType=psirt`** rows—**curl probes must mirror automation identities quarterly alongside Streaming Settings rotations documented beside ND** clusters.
• **Sourcetype drift** —accidental **`KV_MODE=json` overrides stripping `eventType` flatten advisory dashboards—restore bespoke `props.conf` stanzas inside customer-named apps only without rewriting Splunk-supported TA defaults blindly.
• `cisco:eol:bulletin`** duplicates **PSIRT prose referencing lifecycle tables—dedupe `adv_key`** joins **`prod_line`** facets prior to **CAB packets to avoid double-counted `Last Date of Support`** milestones appearing once per bulletin channel only.
grandmaExplanation: We keep vendor security bulletins and hardware lifecycle deadlines lined up with the software versions actually running in your halls so upgrade planners can schedule calmly instead of scrambling once printed support dates quietly pass.
hardware: Cisco Nexus Dashboard appliances providing the NDI advisory engine: physical SE-NODE-G2 / NDA-K9 / NDA1100 (UCS-based) or virtual OVA / KVM / Hyper-V (3-master cluster + worker nodes per scale tier — small / medium / large / extra-large; large-scale typically requires physical appliances). Connected fabrics evaluated against PSIRT/Field-Notice/Bug databases include: Nexus 9000 ACI-mode (leaf/spine — see UC-18.1.* coverage); Nexus 9000 NX-OS standalone (NDFC-managed VXLAN EVPN fabrics with leaf/spine topology); Nexus 7000 / 5000 series under EOL tracking; ASR / NCS routers when paired with NDI cross-platform deployment.
